title: Kanban automation idle worker contract
summary: Kanban semantic discovery and processing remain in Blueprints/Hermes while an explicit producer mode fences the legacy 60-second Blueprints loop and hourly Hermes blocker job from two typed xarta-scheduler targets. Only the nominated singleton owner may run automatic or manual recovery work. Automation exclusion flags opt branches out; root scopes are optional narrowing controls, not required inclusion gates. Retryable Review Processor and preprocessing failures are recorded as durable events and retried with capped backoff. Range-type runtime config values must expose raw, default, accepted range, effective value, source, and state in Automation Status.

singleton_guard.rule: Legacy recurrence, scheduler-provider recurrence, and manual recovery tick processing run only when current_node_id equals an explicit owner_node_id, or when no explicit owner is set and this node's private primary flag is active, unless an operator-created override env/file is active.
singleton_guard.non_owner_result: Return idle_worker_not_owner_node without scanning, queueing, requeueing timeouts, acquiring a lease, claiming markers, or processing work.

tick_serialization.lease_scope: The durable Review Processor lease is acquired before timeout requeue, Review discovery, preprocessing discovery, eligible-marker reads, claims, or model processing and is released after the complete tick.
tick_serialization.run_fencing: Every tick supplies a unique nonempty lease token, including ticks that share the configured holder id or scheduler worker identity.
tick_serialization.competing_tick_result: Return active_lease with lease_acquired=false and explicit skipped scan/requeue results; do not scan, claim, process, or wait for the active tick.
tick_serialization.failure_cleanup: Release the exact token-owned lease when a scan or later phase raises; otherwise retain only the bounded expiry recovery path.

postgres_timeout_contract.pool_timeout: Return HTTP 503 with Retry-After and kanban_postgres_pool_saturated.
postgres_timeout_contract.operation_timeout: Cancel a pending runner future and return HTTP 503 with Retry-After and kanban_postgres_operation_timeout.
postgres_timeout_contract.forbidden_mitigation: Do not increase pool size, command/runner timeout, proxy timeout, or freshness bounds to hide contention.

allowed.legacy: Blueprints lifespan loop runs the 60-second automation tick and the Hermes job runs the hourly blocker tick; scheduler provider is disabled.
allowed.scheduler_shadow: The legacy Blueprints/Hermes recurrence pair remains mutating; scheduler automation performs read-only discovery and scheduler blocker execution is dry-run only.
allowed.scheduler: The Blueprints lifespan loop and Hermes hourly recurrence are fenced; the two typed scheduler targets are the sole automatic recurrence owners.
allowed.fenced: Legacy and scheduler automatic recurrence paths are fenced; owner-node manual recovery remains available.

operator_authorized_preprocessing_recove.scope: one exact ordinary open ToDo leaf
requires: missing readiness marker or clean queued preprocessing marker for the current source
enabled owner-node manual recovery and healthy preprocessing profile
refuses: processing, failed, retry, terminal, stale, or ambiguous target marker
active agent session or processor lease
excluded, non-leaf, non-ToDo, non-item, disabled, non-owner, or unhealthy-profile state
operator_authorized_preprocessing_recove.provenance: Requester identity and authorization reference are retained in durable scan/marker metadata; worker-produced decisions and readiness retain canonical kanban-idle-worker provenance.
non_effects: does not write a client readiness marker
does not start a session or move a lane
does not change scheduler targets, schedules, producer mode, or runtime ownership
does not authorize implementation, commit, deploy, or restart

scope_model.root_item_id_meaning: Optional scan narrowing for manual or configured scoped runs. Empty root means scan all eligible non-excluded Kanban items.
scope_model.exclusion_rule: An item is excluded when the item itself or any ancestor has automation_excluded=true.

max_scan_items.operator_visibility_rule: Do not silently hide an out-of-range env value; report raw_value, default, min, max, effective, source, state, clamped, valid, and error.

candidate_rule.item_status: not archived and not completed unless reprocess_completed=true is explicit

review_processor.queue_rule: Queue when Review text exists and the current Review source hash differs from the last successful/non-retryable processed source hash, unless a current queued, processing, or retry-waiting marker already exists for the same source. Completed cards are skipped unless the caller supplies the named reprocess_completed=true field.
review_processor.feedback_capture_rule: Explicit operator feedback is always appended durably to Review, but capture on a completed card returns skipped_completed_no_reprocess and does not queue a marker unless the request supplies reprocess_completed=true for a genuinely new unresolved requirement.
review_processor.completion_rule: Record a kanban_review_decisions row on success, complete the marker, and store successful processed source hash/date. Retryable failed completion records a failure event and next_retry_at without updating last_successful_source_hash, and clears legacy processed_source_hash/date fields when they match the failed source.

candidate_rule.item_status: open or active, not archived or done

preprocessing_processor.scan_coverage_rule: A bounded global scan must not repeatedly select the same current marked leaves. Missing readiness remains first priority; marked candidates advance through the latest successful serialized global scan audit so every eligible ToDo leaf is eventually rebuilt against the complete normalized context manifest without per-item heartbeat writes. Cursor lineage remains in scan_cursor_previous_audit_id, but lookup must use the indexed latest-action ordering and must not self-join the audit ledger or parse successor JSON.
preprocessing_processor.scan_database_boundary: Cursor lookup, candidate selection, context rebuilding, and marker writes remain inside the existing measured synchronous scan worker and Kanban connection boundary reached through _run_personal_sync_work. No synchronous SQLite or PostgreSQL work is added to the async route or Blueprints event loop.
preprocessing_processor.queue_rule: Queue when an eligible ToDo leaf is not backed by a processed preprocessing marker for the exact current normalized source hash, unless a current queued or processing marker already exists. A fresh context_readiness_marker in agent hints is semantic evidence, not an operational readiness attestation by itself.
preprocessing_processor.operator_authorized_recovery_rule: An explicit current operator request to work one named leaf may use the dedicated recovery surface only for the initial missing-readiness case or a clean queued marker for the exact source. The server rechecks candidacy, owner/manual recovery, profile health, sessions, lease, marker state, and source; it forces one preprocessing-only scan/claim and preserves requester/authorization metadata separately from canonical worker provenance. It never writes readiness directly or bypasses failure, retry, awaiting_operator, stale, terminal, exclusion, lane, session, schedule, target, or ownership guards.
preprocessing_processor.ready_result: Persist the accepted context_readiness_marked decision and fresh context_readiness_marker for the exact marker id and normalized source hash, then complete the processor marker with matching processed and last-successful source hashes.
preprocessing_processor.ready_leaf_no_decomposition_rule: If the configured LLM route returns ready=false but supplies no blocking codes, no operator question, and no decomposition_items for an otherwise concrete actionable leaf, normalize the outcome to ready_leaf_no_decomposition and complete the marker successfully. Readiness is not completion, and retrying the same source should not be used as a substitute for doing the named investigation or implementation work.
preprocessing_processor.decomposition_result: Create or confirm concrete child cards, move the former ToDo parent out of ToDo, write readiness marker and decision proof, then complete the marker.
preprocessing_processor.blocked_result: Create a visible blocker or question on the smallest useful leaf, write decision proof, and fail the marker visibly as retryable queue state unless the operator/work state makes it terminal.

retry_policy.processed_source_hash_rule: Retryable failed attempts must not update last_successful_source_hash and must not leave processed_source_hash equal to the failed source.
retry_policy.status_summary_rule: Automation Status summary fields last_error, next_retry_at, retry_waiting_count, and retry_due_count describe active failed/retry markers only. Historical failure events and aggregates remain durable proof and expose scheduled_retry_at/historical_last_error where needed, but recovered processed markers must not present old retry times as active work.
retry_policy.retention_rule: Failure events are retained for diagnostics until an operator deliberately prunes them. POST /api/v1/personal/kanban/automation/failures/prune previews by default and, when apply=true, deletes matched inactive/recovered failure events while skipping active retry markers unless include_active=true is explicitly supplied.

rules.condition: queued, processing, or failed marker source is stale
rules.action: re-scan and requeue current source or cancel old marker

rules.condition: fresh readiness marker and exact accepted real-preprocessor decision exist for the same item, processor marker id, normalized source hash, worker schema, configured profile/provider mode, and worker provenance, but a queued/failed marker or the legacy preprocessing_current cancellation shows finalization was interrupted
rules.action: reconcile the existing marker to processed, clear active failure/retry state, resolve its synthetic blocker, and retain the durable decision and failure history

rules.condition: fresh readiness marker exists without an exact accepted real-preprocessor decision and processed marker
rules.action: keep or enqueue genuine preprocessing work; never cancel or mark processed from the agent hint alone

rules.condition: Hermes processor profile, auth, or model route is missing/unavailable
rules.action: record retryable failure event; do not perform deterministic substitute processing

must_pass_tests: No root env var set: idle worker still scans automatically.
automation_excluded=true on a root or topic ancestor: no descendants are scanned, queued, claimed, or processed.
GitHub Activity marked automation_excluded: no blockers, ToDos, or preprocessing markers are created there.
New ToDo leaf with missing readiness marker: preprocessing marker is queued and processed.

A direct operator-authorized exact-leaf recovery with only missing/clean-queued preprocessing state runs one preprocessing-only pass, retains requester/authorization evidence, and never claims a Review marker or writes readiness directly.

Operator-authorized recovery refuses active processing/lease/session, retry or failed/terminal/stale marker state, current/stale/awaiting_operator readiness, non-candidate/excluded state, non-owner/manual fence, or unhealthy preprocessor profile.
Backlog, Doing, Blocked, Done, archived, and non-leaf cards are not preprocessing candidates.
Fresh readiness marker plus processed marker and unchanged normalized source hashes: no reprocessing.
Fresh readiness marker without an exact accepted preprocessing decision and processed marker remains queued for a genuine pass; the agent hint alone never bypasses strict readiness.

An interrupted failed or cancelled marker is reconciled to processed only when an exact accepted real-preprocessor decision matches item, marker id, normalized source hash, schema, profile/provider mode, affected ref, and worker provenance.
Explicit operator, exclusion, or other terminal cancellation is never reconciled from decision evidence and remains terminal for the unchanged source.
Attestations with mismatched source hash, marker id, schema, profile/provider mode, affected ref, provenance, or ready state are rejected.
Decision evidence for a scan batch is loaded with one bounded batched query rather than per-card lookup.
Successive bounded global scans advance an audited cursor across marked candidates, so a link or other context-only change on an older leaf cannot starve behind the same current batch.
The scan cursor and all Kanban database work remain inside the measured synchronous worker/connection boundary; the async route performs no synchronous database work.
Detail, Review, proof, or explicit feedback capture on a Done card does not queue Review Processor or preprocessing work unless the named reprocess_completed field is true.
Actionable leaf with no blocker/question/decomposition from local AI is marked ready_leaf_no_decomposition, not failed.
Body/detail/review/discussion/tree/link/blocker/commit/todo/count drift makes readiness stale and requeues preprocessing.
Old queued marker from invalid/global bad run is cancelled instead of processed.
Missing or unavailable Hermes processor profile/auth/model route fails visibly as retryable failure state; deterministic substitute processing is forbidden.
Retryable Review Processor/preprocessing failure does not update last_successful_source_hash and clears processed_source_hash if it matches the failed source.
Automation Status exposes failure events, repeated failure aggregates, attempt count, last error, next retry time, and retry-waiting/due state.
After a retry succeeds, Automation Status keeps historical failure events but clears active retry summary fields.
Source changes supersede retry wait and queue fresh work for the new source hash.
A non-owner node returns idle_worker_not_owner_node and does not scan, queue, requeue, lease, claim, or process idle work.
